title: Systems and devices for optical power transmission
abstract: An optical voltage transformer (OVT) includes a light-emitting device (100b) including an array of light-emitters (140) arranged as a plurality of groups of light-emitters (145), each light-emitter group (145) being individually addressable. The OVT further includes a photovoltaic device (200b) configured to convert received light into electrical power and including an array of photovoltaic cells (240) configured to face the array of light-emitters (100b). The array of photovoltaic cells (200b) is arranged as a plurality groups (245) of photovoltaic cells (240), each group comprising a plurality of photovoltaic cells (240) electrically connected in series. The array of photovoltaic cells (200b) has a one-to-one correspondence with the array of light-emitters (100b) so that each photovoltaic cell (240) is configured to receive light emitted from a corresponding light-emitter (140).
claims:
Claims 1 . An optical voltage trans former, comprising : a light-emitting device comprising an array of lightemitters deposed on a substrate arranged as a plurality of groups of light-emitters , each group being individually addressable ; a photovoltaic device configured to convert received light into electrical power comprising an array of photovoltaic cells disposed on a front surface of a photovoltaic substrate and configured to face the array of light-emitters , the array of photovoltaic cells arranged as a plurality groups of photovoltaic cell groups , each group comprising a plurality of photovoltaic cells electrically connected in series ; and an optical transmission medium disposed between the light-emitting device and the photovoltaic device ; wherein the array of photovoltaic cells has a one-to-one correspondence with the array of light-emitters so that each photovoltaic cell is configured to receive light emitted from a corresponding light-emitter . 2 . The optical voltage trans former of claim 1 , wherein the plurality of groups of light-emitters has a one-to-one correspondence with the plurality of groups of photovoltaic cells so that each of the plurality of groups of the light-emitters , when individually addressed is configured to emit light to be received by a corresponding group of photovoltaic cells of the plurality of groups of photovoltaic cells . 3 . The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 1 or 2 , wherein each group of the plurality of groups of lightemitters comprises light-emitters apertures having a physical arrangement , and wherein each group of the plurality of groups of photovoltaic cells comprises photovoltaic cells in a physical arrangement that matches or mirrors the physical arrangement of the light-emitters of the corresponding group of lightemitters . 4 . The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 1 to 3 , wherein the array of light-emitting apertures is in a two-dimensional , 2D, arrangement , and wherein the array of photovoltaic apertures is in a two- dimensional , 2D, arrangement . 5. The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 1 to 4 , wherein each photovoltaic cell comprises photovoltaic aperture having a layered structure , the layered structure comprising : a first semiconductor layer of a first conductivity type , and having a cathode contact disposed partially on a front surface of the first semiconductor layer ; an anode semiconductor layer disposed on the front surface of the substrate and having an electrical contact with the anode contact ; and a second semiconductor layer of a second conductivity type that is di f ferent from the first conductivity type , the second semiconductor layer disposed between the first semiconductor and the anode layer . 6. The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 1 to 5 , wherein each light-emitter comprises a light-emitting aperture . 7 . The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 1 to 6 , wherein the photovoltaic device comprising an anode and a cathode ; wherein the plurality of groups of photovoltaic cells are electrically coupled in parallel between the anode and the cathode . 8 . The optical voltage trans former of claim 7 , wherein each group of the plurality of groups of photovoltaic cells comprises one or more interconnects to electrically connect the plurality of photovoltaic cells of the group in series . 9. The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 7 or 8 , wherein for each of the plurality of groups of photovoltaic cells , a first of the serially connected photovoltaic cells is electrically coupled to the cathode of the photovoltaic device and a last of the serially connected photovoltaic cells is electrically coupled to the anode of the photovoltaic device . 10 . The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 1 to 9 , wherein at least one of the plurality of groups of photovoltaic cells comprises photovoltaic cells physically 

arranged and serially connected linearly in a single dimension . 11 . The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 1 to 10 , wherein at least one of the plurality of groups of photovoltaic cells comprises photovoltaic cells has a physical layout along two dimensions . 12 . The optical voltage trans former of claim 11 , wherein the at least one group of photovoltaic cells is arranged in a 2D rectangular array . 13 . The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 11 or 12 , wherein the at least one group of photovoltaic apertures arranged in a 2D square array . 14 . The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 11 to 13 , wherein the at least one group of photovoltaic cells has a physical arranged along two dimensions and is serially connected in a serpentine-like manner . 15 . The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 11 to 14 , wherein the at least one group of photovoltaic cells has a physical arrangement along two dimensions and is serially connected in a meandering or serpentine-meandering manner . 16 . The optical voltage trans former of any one of claims 10 to 15 , 

wherein all of the plurality of groups of photovoltaic apertures are electrically serially in with a same shape. 17. The optical voltage transformer of claim 16, wherein all of the plurality of groups of photovoltaic cells have the same number of photovoltaic cells therein. 18. The optical voltage transformer of any of claims 1 to 17, wherein the light-emitting device further comprises: a common cathode, and a plurality of anodes respectively for the plurality of groups of light-emitters. 19. The optical voltage transformer of claim 18, wherein the light-emitters of each light-emitter group are electrically connected in parallel between the common cathode and the respective anode. 20. The optical voltage transformer of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein the light-emitting device is a vertical cavity surface emitting laser (VCSEL) device and the light-emitters comprise VCSEL apertures.
